# BR-001 Gherkin Spec — FR-3 & FR-4 (Deferred Requirements Now Resolved)
# Generated by: Gherkin Spec Agent
# Source: BR-001-portfolio-tracking.md + decisions 2026-05-08
# Stack: FastAPI (pytest + httpx)
# Uncle Bob rule: Tests MUST fail before production code is written

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS LOCKED
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# FR-3 NAV Snapshot:
#   Trigger 1: APScheduler fires daily at 18:00 WAT (15:00 UTC)
#   Trigger 2: Any price update (quick entry OR PDF upload) triggers
#              a snapshot if one has not already been taken today
#   Idempotent: max one snapshot per calendar day (UNIQUE on snapshot_date)
#   Manual trigger: POST /api/v1/nav-history/snapshot (admin only)
#
# FR-4 XIRR:
#   Computed per holding across all BUY transactions for that holding
#   Shown alongside simple return% in Holdings response
#   Field name: "xirr_pct" (string, e.g. "+18.42" or null if < 2 transactions)
#   Minimum data requirement: at least 2 transaction records with dates
#   If insufficient data: xirr_pct = null (not an error)
#   Library: scipy.optimize.brentq (no new pip dependency beyond scipy)
#
# FR-12 Multi-portfolio: deferred to Phase 4. No spec needed now.

---

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FEATURE FILE 5 — NAV History Snapshots
# Covers: FR-3
# File: features/nav_history.feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Feature: NAV history snapshots
  As a portfolio owner
  I want my portfolio value recorded daily
  So that I can track performance over time

  Background:
    Given I am authenticated as an admin user
    And the nav_history table is empty for today

  Scenario: SC-025 Manual snapshot trigger creates a nav_history record
    Given active holdings exist with a total current value of "45000.00"
    And total cost basis across all active holdings is "40000.00"
    When I POST /api/v1/nav-history/snapshot
    Then the response status is 200
    And a nav_history record exists for today with total_value "45000.00"
    And the record has total_cost "40000.00"
    And the record has gain_loss "5000.00"

  Scenario: SC-026 Snapshot is idempotent — second trigger on same day updates not duplicates
    Given a nav_history record already exists for today with total_value "45000.00"
    When I POST /api/v1/nav-history/snapshot
    Then the response status is 200
    And exactly one nav_history record exists for today
    And the record has the latest computed total_value

  Scenario: SC-027 Price update triggers a snapshot if none exists today
    Given no nav_history record exists for today
    And DANGCEM has current_price "450.00"
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/quick with a valid price update
    Then a nav_history record is created for today automatically
    And the record reflects the portfolio value after the price update

  Scenario: SC-028 Price update does not create duplicate snapshot if one exists today
    Given a nav_history record already exists for today
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/quick with a valid price update
    Then exactly one nav_history record exists for today

  Scenario: SC-029 NAV history list returns records in chronological order
    Given nav_history records exist for the past 5 days
    When I GET /api/v1/nav-history
    Then the response status is 200
    And the records are ordered by snapshot_date ascending
    And each record contains total_value, total_cost, and gain_loss as strings

  Scenario: SC-030 Readonly user can view NAV history but cannot trigger snapshot
    Given I am authenticated as a readonly user
    When I GET /api/v1/nav-history
    Then the response status is 200
    When I POST /api/v1/nav-history/snapshot
    Then the response status is 403

  Scenario: SC-031 Snapshot only includes live active holdings not claims or drafts
    Given a live active holding with current_value "45000.00"
    And a draft holding with current_value "10000.00"
    And a claim holding with current_value "0.00"
    When I POST /api/v1/nav-history/snapshot
    Then the nav_history record has total_value "45000.00"
    And draft and claim values are excluded from the snapshot

---

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FEATURE FILE 6 — XIRR Annualised Return
# Covers: FR-4
# File: features/xirr.feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Feature: XIRR annualised return per holding
  As a portfolio owner
  I want to see the annualised return for each holding
  So that I can compare performance across investments made at different times

  Background:
    Given I am authenticated as an admin user

  Scenario: SC-032 XIRR computed when two or more buy transactions exist
    Given a holding for DANGCEM with current_price "500.00" and num_shares 100
    And transaction records for DANGCEM:
      | date       | type | shares | price_per_share |
      | 2024-01-15 | buy  | 60     | 380.00          |
      | 2024-07-01 | buy  | 40     | 420.00          |
    When I GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the holding for DANGCEM has "xirr_pct" that is not null
    And "xirr_pct" is a string representing a percentage
    And "xirr_pct" is different from "return_pct"

  Scenario: SC-033 XIRR is null when fewer than two transactions exist
    Given a holding for GTCO with only one buy transaction
    When I GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the holding for GTCO has "xirr_pct" equal to null

  Scenario: SC-034 XIRR is null when no transaction records exist for a holding
    Given a holding for ZENITHBANK with no linked transaction records
    When I GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the holding for ZENITHBANK has "xirr_pct" equal to null

  Scenario: SC-035 XIRR is shown alongside simple return_pct not replacing it
    Given a holding with two or more buy transactions
    When I GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then each holding has both "return_pct" and "xirr_pct" fields in the response
    And "return_pct" is the simple return (current_value - cost_basis) / cost_basis

  Scenario: SC-036 Claim holdings always have null xirr_pct
    Given a claim holding for a defunct company with two transactions
    When I GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the claim holding has "xirr_pct" equal to null
    And the claim holding has "return_pct" equal to null

  Scenario: SC-037 xirr_pct is a string not a float in API response
    Given a holding with sufficient transactions to compute XIRR
    When I GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the "xirr_pct" field is a JSON string
    And the "xirr_pct" field is not a JSON number

---

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PYTEST TESTS — FR-3 NAV Snapshot
# File: backend/tests/integration/test_fr3_nav_snapshot.py
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

"""
Generated from: features/nav_history.feature
Uncle Bob rule: Run these first — confirm RED — then write production code.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date
from decimal import Decimal
from httpx import AsyncClient


class TestNavSnapshot:

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc025_manual_snapshot_creates_nav_record(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_live_holding, test_company, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: nav_history.feature | SC-025"""
        test_company.current_price = Decimal("450.00")
        test_live_holding.num_shares = 100
        test_live_holding.avg_purchase_price = Decimal("400.00")
        await db_session.flush()

        response = await admin_http_client.post("/api/v1/nav-history/snapshot")

        # Spec: SC-025 | Then response status 200
        assert response.status_code == 200

        from sqlalchemy import select, text
        result = await db_session.execute(
            text("SELECT total_value, total_cost, gain_loss FROM nav_history "
                 "WHERE snapshot_date = CURRENT_DATE")
        )
        row = result.fetchone()
        # Spec: SC-025 | And nav_history record exists for today
        assert row is not None
        # Spec: SC-025 | And total_value == 45000.00
        assert str(row.total_value) == "45000.00"
        # Spec: SC-025 | And total_cost == 40000.00
        assert str(row.total_cost) == "40000.00"
        # Spec: SC-025 | And gain_loss == 5000.00
        assert str(row.gain_loss) == "5000.00"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc026_snapshot_is_idempotent_same_day(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: nav_history.feature | SC-026"""
        from sqlalchemy import text

        # First snapshot
        await admin_http_client.post("/api/v1/nav-history/snapshot")
        # Second snapshot same day
        response = await admin_http_client.post("/api/v1/nav-history/snapshot")

        assert response.status_code == 200

        result = await db_session.execute(
            text("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nav_history WHERE snapshot_date = CURRENT_DATE")
        )
        count = result.scalar_one()
        # Spec: SC-026 | Then exactly one record exists for today
        assert count == 1

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc027_price_update_triggers_snapshot_if_none_today(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_company, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: nav_history.feature | SC-027"""
        from sqlalchemy import text

        # Confirm no snapshot exists
        result = await db_session.execute(
            text("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nav_history WHERE snapshot_date = CURRENT_DATE")
        )
        assert result.scalar_one() == 0

        # Price update
        await admin_http_client.post(
            "/api/v1/prices/quick",
            json={
                "company_id": test_company.id,
                "price": "450.00",
                "entry_date": str(date.today()),
            }
        )

        result = await db_session.execute(
            text("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nav_history WHERE snapshot_date = CURRENT_DATE")
        )
        count = result.scalar_one()
        # Spec: SC-027 | Then snapshot was auto-created
        assert count == 1

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc028_price_update_no_duplicate_snapshot(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_company, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: nav_history.feature | SC-028"""
        from sqlalchemy import text

        # Create snapshot first
        await admin_http_client.post("/api/v1/nav-history/snapshot")

        # Then do a price update
        await admin_http_client.post(
            "/api/v1/prices/quick",
            json={"company_id": test_company.id, "price": "460.00",
                  "entry_date": str(date.today())}
        )

        result = await db_session.execute(
            text("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nav_history WHERE snapshot_date = CURRENT_DATE")
        )
        count = result.scalar_one()
        # Spec: SC-028 | Then still exactly one record
        assert count == 1

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc029_nav_history_returns_records_chronologically(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: nav_history.feature | SC-029"""
        from sqlalchemy import text
        from datetime import timedelta

        for i in range(5):
            day = date.today() - timedelta(days=4 - i)
            await db_session.execute(
                text("INSERT INTO nav_history (snapshot_date, total_value, total_cost) "
                     "VALUES (:d, '10000.00', '9000.00') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING"),
                {"d": day}
            )
        await db_session.flush()

        response = await admin_http_client.get("/api/v1/nav-history")
        assert response.status_code == 200

        records = response.json()["data"]
        dates = [r["snapshot_date"] for r in records]
        # Spec: SC-029 | Then records ordered ascending by date
        assert dates == sorted(dates)
        # Spec: SC-029 | And each record has required string fields
        for r in records:
            for field in ("total_value", "total_cost", "gain_loss"):
                assert isinstance(r[field], str)

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc030_readonly_can_view_but_not_trigger_snapshot(
        self, readonly_http_client: AsyncClient
    ):
        """Spec: nav_history.feature | SC-030"""
        # Spec: SC-030 | GET allowed
        get_response = await readonly_http_client.get("/api/v1/nav-history")
        assert get_response.status_code == 200

        # Spec: SC-030 | POST blocked
        post_response = await readonly_http_client.post("/api/v1/nav-history/snapshot")
        assert post_response.status_code == 403

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc031_snapshot_excludes_drafts_and_claims(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient,
        test_live_holding, test_draft_holding, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: nav_history.feature | SC-031"""
        from sqlalchemy import text

        # Set prices
        from sqlalchemy import select
        from app.models import Company

        # Make test_live_holding active with known value
        test_live_holding.num_shares = 100
        test_live_holding.holding_type = "active"
        test_live_holding.status = "live"
        test_draft_holding.status = "draft"
        await db_session.flush()

        await admin_http_client.post("/api/v1/nav-history/snapshot")

        result = await db_session.execute(
            text("SELECT total_value FROM nav_history WHERE snapshot_date = CURRENT_DATE")
        )
        row = result.fetchone()
        assert row is not None

        # Spec: SC-031 | Draft holdings excluded from snapshot total
        # The draft holding's value must not be included
        # (exact value assertion depends on test fixtures' prices)
        assert row.total_value is not None


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PYTEST TESTS — FR-4 XIRR
# File: backend/tests/integration/test_fr4_xirr.py
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

"""
Generated from: features/xirr.feature
scipy must be in requirements.txt before these tests can pass.
Add: scipy==1.13.1
"""


class TestXIRR:

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc032_xirr_computed_with_two_or_more_transactions(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_company, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: xirr.feature | SC-032"""
        from app.models import Holding, Transaction

        test_company.current_price = Decimal("500.00")
        holding = Holding(
            company_id=test_company.id,
            num_shares=100,
            avg_purchase_price=Decimal("395.00"),
            holding_type="active",
            status="live",
        )
        db_session.add(holding)
        await db_session.flush()

        for tx_date, shares, price in [
            (date(2024, 1, 15), 60, Decimal("380.00")),
            (date(2024, 7, 1), 40, Decimal("420.00")),
        ]:
            db_session.add(Transaction(
                holding_id=holding.id,
                transaction_date=tx_date,
                ticker=test_company.ticker,
                transaction_type="buy",
                num_shares=shares,
                price_per_share=price,
                net_amount=shares * price,
                status="live",
            ))
        await db_session.flush()

        response = await admin_http_client.get("/api/v1/holdings")
        assert response.status_code == 200

        data = response.json()["data"]
        h = next(h for h in data if h["id"] == holding.id)

        # Spec: SC-032 | Then xirr_pct is not null
        assert h["xirr_pct"] is not None
        # Spec: SC-032 | And xirr_pct is a string
        assert isinstance(h["xirr_pct"], str)
        # Spec: SC-032 | And xirr_pct differs from return_pct
        assert h["xirr_pct"] != h["return_pct"]

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc033_xirr_null_with_fewer_than_two_transactions(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_company, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: xirr.feature | SC-033"""
        from app.models import Holding, Transaction

        holding = Holding(
            company_id=test_company.id,
            num_shares=50,
            avg_purchase_price=Decimal("300.00"),
            holding_type="active",
            status="live",
        )
        db_session.add(holding)
        await db_session.flush()

        db_session.add(Transaction(
            holding_id=holding.id,
            transaction_date=date(2024, 1, 1),
            ticker=test_company.ticker,
            transaction_type="buy",
            num_shares=50,
            price_per_share=Decimal("300.00"),
            net_amount=Decimal("15000.00"),
            status="live",
        ))
        await db_session.flush()

        response = await admin_http_client.get("/api/v1/holdings")
        data = response.json()["data"]
        h = next(h for h in data if h["id"] == holding.id)

        # Spec: SC-033 | Then xirr_pct is null (only one transaction)
        assert h["xirr_pct"] is None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc034_xirr_null_with_no_transactions(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_live_holding
    ):
        """Spec: xirr.feature | SC-034"""
        response = await admin_http_client.get("/api/v1/holdings")
        data = response.json()["data"]
        h = next(h for h in data if h["id"] == test_live_holding.id)

        # Spec: SC-034 | Then xirr_pct is null (no transactions)
        assert h["xirr_pct"] is None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc035_both_return_pct_and_xirr_pct_present(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_live_holding
    ):
        """Spec: xirr.feature | SC-035"""
        response = await admin_http_client.get("/api/v1/holdings")
        data = response.json()["data"]

        for h in data:
            # Spec: SC-035 | Then both fields present in every holding
            assert "return_pct" in h, f"return_pct missing from holding {h.get('id')}"
            assert "xirr_pct" in h, f"xirr_pct missing from holding {h.get('id')}"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc036_claim_holding_has_null_xirr(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_company, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: xirr.feature | SC-036"""
        from app.models import Holding, Transaction

        claim_holding = Holding(
            company_id=test_company.id,
            num_shares=200,
            avg_purchase_price=Decimal("5.00"),
            holding_type="claim",
            cost_basis_override=Decimal("0.00"),
            status="live",
        )
        db_session.add(claim_holding)
        await db_session.flush()

        for tx_date, price in [(date(2020, 1, 1), Decimal("8.00")),
                               (date(2021, 6, 1), Decimal("4.00"))]:
            db_session.add(Transaction(
                holding_id=claim_holding.id,
                transaction_date=tx_date,
                ticker=test_company.ticker,
                transaction_type="buy",
                num_shares=100,
                price_per_share=price,
                net_amount=100 * price,
                status="live",
            ))
        await db_session.flush()

        response = await admin_http_client.get("/api/v1/holdings")
        data = response.json()["data"]
        h = next(h for h in data if h["id"] == claim_holding.id)

        # Spec: SC-036 | Then claim holding xirr_pct is null
        assert h["xirr_pct"] is None
        # Spec: SC-036 | And return_pct is null
        assert h["return_pct"] is None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_sc037_xirr_pct_is_string_not_float(
        self, admin_http_client: AsyncClient, test_company, db_session
    ):
        """Spec: xirr.feature | SC-037"""
        from app.models import Holding, Transaction

        holding = Holding(
            company_id=test_company.id,
            num_shares=100,
            avg_purchase_price=Decimal("400.00"),
            holding_type="active",
            status="live",
        )
        db_session.add(holding)
        await db_session.flush()

        for tx_date, price in [(date(2024, 1, 1), Decimal("380.00")),
                               (date(2024, 6, 1), Decimal("420.00"))]:
            db_session.add(Transaction(
                holding_id=holding.id,
                transaction_date=tx_date,
                ticker=test_company.ticker,
                transaction_type="buy",
                num_shares=50,
                price_per_share=price,
                net_amount=50 * price,
                status="live",
            ))
        await db_session.flush()

        test_company.current_price = Decimal("500.00")
        await db_session.flush()

        response = await admin_http_client.get("/api/v1/holdings")
        data = response.json()["data"]
        h = next(h for h in data if h["id"] == holding.id)

        if h["xirr_pct"] is not None:
            # Spec: SC-037 | Then xirr_pct is a JSON string not a number
            assert isinstance(h["xirr_pct"], str)

# NEW DEPENDENCIES — add to backend/requirements.txt
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# scipy==1.13.1   # XIRR computation via scipy.optimize.brentq
#
# XIRR implementation reference for app/services/portfolio.py:
#
# from scipy.optimize import brentq
# from datetime import date
# from decimal import Decimal
#
# def calculate_xirr(
#     transactions: list[tuple[date, Decimal]],  # (date, cashflow) pairs
#     current_value: Decimal,
#     current_date: date,
# ) -> Decimal | None:
#     """
#     transactions: list of (date, amount) where buy = negative cashflow
#     e.g. [(2024-01-15, -22800.00), (2024-07-01, -16800.00)]
#     current_value: today's market value = positive cashflow
#     Returns: annualised return as Decimal or None if < 2 cash flows
#     """
#     if len(transactions) < 2:
#         return None
#
#     cashflows = [(d, float(-amt)) for d, amt in transactions]
#     cashflows.append((current_date, float(current_value)))
#
#     min_date = min(d for d, _ in cashflows)
#     years = [(d - min_date).days / 365.25 for d, _ in cashflows]
#     amounts = [cf for _, cf in cashflows]
#
#     def npv(rate):
#         return sum(amt / (1 + rate) ** t for amt, t in zip(amounts, years))
#
#     try:
#         xirr = brentq(npv, -0.999, 10.0)
#         return Decimal(str(round(xirr * 100, 2)))
#     except (ValueError, RuntimeError):
#         return None
